mnemonics: Greedy Grab: Always take the best available option right now without worrying about the future consequences.
keyPoints: Makes the locally optimal choice at each stage with the hope of finding a global optimum.
Used for optimization problems where a greedy choice leads to an optimal solution.
Requires proof of correctness; a greedy algorithm doesn't work for every problem.
commonMistakes: Assuming a greedy solution is always optimal without proving it (e.g., Coin Change problem with arbitrary denominations).
Confusing Greedy with Dynamic Programming; Greedy never reconsiders choices, while DP does.
